toolInput:
{"description":"Explore withAuth and API routes","prompt":"Explore the codebase to understand:
1. The withAuth HOC at src/auth/withAuth.ts — read it fully
2. All route files in src/app/api/ — list them all and read key ones:
   - Any route that DOES NOT use withAuth
   - notes/route.ts, notes/[id]/route.ts, quizzes/[id]/attempts/route.ts, quizzes/[id]/submit/route.ts, csrf-token/route.ts, health/route.ts, courses/search/route.ts
3. How roles are used in withAuth (what roles are defined, what RbacRole looks like)
4. What the notes/[id]/route.ts looks like — specifically the GET handler and any redundant guards

Return:
- Full content of withAuth.ts
- List of all API route files with whether they use withAuth
- Content of notes/route.ts, notes/[id]/route.ts, csrf-token/route.ts, health/route.ts, quizzes/[id]/attempts/route.ts, quizzes/[id]/submit/route.ts
- What the "optional" flag does in withAuth
- What roles are supported","subagent_type":"Explore"}

Now I have a complete picture. Let me compile the findings and output the classification:

- `health/route.ts` — intentionally public ✅
- `csrf-token/route.ts` — intentionally unauthenticated CSRF factory (uses `x-session-id`, not JWT)
